sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 10.65 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.10)

statement.latex: Determine the structure of infinite 2-transitive permutation groups$(G, )$in which the stabilizer of a point$$has the form$G_{ }=A G_{ }$where$G _{ }$is the stabilizer of two points$,$,$$, such that$G _{ }$contains an element inverting the subgroup$A$. Suppose, in particular, that$A \{ 1\}$contains at most two conjugacy classes of$G_{ }$; does$G$then possess a normal subgroup isomorphic to$PSL_2$o...
